world.title: Hin Leong founder O.K. Lim hospitalised three days before 13½-year jail sentence starts
world.description: SINGAPORE: Hin Leong founder Lim Oon Kuin was hospitalised with breathing difficulties just three days before the 84-year-old's 13½-year prison sentence was due to start on April 1.

world.title: Why has Spain closed its airspace to US planes amid the Iran war?
world.description: Spain has closed its airspace to US planes involved in the Iran conflict. This reaffirms Madrid’s opposition to the war, with Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez calling Washington’s strikes ‘an unjustified and dangerous military intervention that is outside of international law’. The move is likely to drive a bigger wedge between the two Nato allies

world.title: Israel approves death penalty for Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks
world.description: The bill would make the death penalty the default punishment for Palestinians in the Israeli-occupied West Bank found guilty of intentionally carrying out deadly attacks deemed "acts of terrorism" by an Israeli military court.

world.title: They lied, they're dead: White House mocks slain Iran leaders over failed talks
world.description: Explosive remarks from the White House link the deaths of Iranian leaders to failed negotiations, even as backchannel talks continue and threats escalate, raising fresh questions over the direction of the month-long war.

world.title: Iran Update Special Report, March 30, 2026
world.description: US President Donald Trump told the Financial Times on March 30 that the combined force has struck 13,000 targets since the start of the war
world.source: Institute for the Study of War

world.title: What caused the blood red skies in Western Australia? A weather expert explains
world.description: The northwest of Australia is one of only a few places in the world that experience tropical cyclones in the desert.

world.title: AfDB says Africa's growth risks were tilted to downside even before Iran conflict
world.description: The shock of the Middle Eastern conflict to the African economy could be modest, the African Development Bank (AfDB) said on Monday, although it worsens an already weakened outlook, with debt burdens, shrinking aid, and global instability.

world.title: Iran Approves Strait Of Hormuz Plan With Tolls For Vessels, Bans US, Israeli Ships
world.description: Iran parliament backs new Strait of Hormuz plan with ship tolls, bans on US and Israeli vessels, and tighter rules for countries enforcing sanctions

business.title: Australia to ban surcharges on payment cards, cut bank fees
business.description: The central bank estimated the change would save consumers A$1.6 billion (S$1.42 billion) a year. Read more at straitstimes.com.

business.title: Malaysia Raises Growth Outlook, Sees War Impact Contained
business.description: Malaysia lifted its economic growth forecast for 2026, expecting strong domestic demand and investment to cushion the impact of the escalating war in the Middle East.

business.title: China Targets Offshore Trusts in Tax Crackdown on Ultra-Rich
business.description: Chinese officials are intensifying efforts to tax offshore trusts that hold shares in some Hong Kong-listed companies, clamping down on a structure that the country’s mega-rich have used to invest billions of dollars overseas.

business.title: Australia tightens merger rules from April as ACCC thresholds expand
business.description: Australia’s new mandatory merger control regime will enter its next major phase this week, on 1 April, introducing additional notification thresholds and voting power rules that expand the number of transactions requiring clearance.

business.title: Hin Leong founder O.K. Lim hospitalised three days before 13½-year jail sentence starts
business.description: SINGAPORE: Hin Leong founder Lim Oon Kuin was hospitalised with breathing difficulties just three days before the 84-year-old's 13½-year prison sentence was due to start on April 1.

business.title: Australia: Glencore threatens tiered wages at Mangoola coal mine
business.description: The financial press has noted that this could set a precedent for the slashing of labour-hire wages throughout the coal mining industry and more broadly.

business.title: New Swisslog APeC structure delivers greater resources to Southeast Asian customers
business.description: Experienced leader, Steven Xie, to serve as Executive Vice President & Managing Director, based in Malaysia

business.title: Gold set for worst month in more than 17 years as U.S. rate-cut hopes fade
business.description: Gold climbed Tuesday on hopes of de-escalation in the Middle East war, as higher energy prices dimmed hopes for a U.S. interest rate cut this year.

science.title: New Tool Deciphers Fungal Gene Functions from RNA
science.description: Researchers have developed a new computational tool des […]

science.title: A surprising new idea about how the Big Bang may have happened
science.description: Scientists at the University of Waterloo have uncovered a bold new way to explain how the universe began—one that could reshape our understanding of the Big Bang. Instead of relying on patched-together theories, their approach shows that the universe’s explosive early growth may arise naturally from a deeper framework called quantum gravity.

science.title: SpaceX's Starlink loses contact with satellite, says no threat to space missions
science.description: SpaceX's Starlink reports communication loss with one of its satellites, but assures that it poses no risk to key missions, including NASA's Artemis II moon mission and International Space Station operations. The company is actively investigating the anomaly and coordinating with U.S. Space Force.

science.title: LIGO May Have Detected The First Primordial Black Hole, Scientists Say
science.description: Primordial black holes (PBHs) are particularly special types of black hole, thought to have been formed from dense pockets of subatomic matter within a second after the Big Bang – and a new study reports on what could be the first direct observation of one.

science.title: LIGO May Have Detected The First Primordial Black Hole, Scientists Say
Why it's 'almost impossible to track' meteoroids in space, NASA explains
science.description: After Ohio fireball sparks panic, NASA says meteor surge is normal.

science.title: SMILE mission to be launched on April 9
science.description: All prelaunch activities for the China-Europe joint mission, the Solar wind Magnetosphere Ionosphere Link Explorer (SMILE), have been completed at Europe's spaceport in French Guiana, South America, and the launch is scheduled for April 9, the National Space Science Center of the Chinese Academy of Sciences announced recently.

science.title: Countdown begins for Artemis II moon mission with Canadian astronaut, NASA says
science.description: Weather forecasts look favourable for target launch date on Wednesday

ai.title: Why Demis Hassabis chose Google over Facebook despite bigger offer from Mark Zuckerberg
ai.description: Demis Hassabis, CEO of Google’s AI division, turned down a lucrative offer from Facebook, choosing instead to join Larry Page, highlighting his focus on long-term vision over immediate financial gain.

ai.title: More than half of US says AI likely to harm them, poll finds
ai.description: Seventy per cent of Americans think advancements in AI are likely to reduce job opportunities. Read more at straitstimes.com.

ai.title: Trump helped build the Middle East’s AI ambitions. Could his war break them?
ai.description: By John Liu, Lisa Eadicicco, CNN Hong Kong/New York (CNN) — President Donald Trump arrived in the Middle East last spring, making deals that would vault the Gulf into the global race for artificial intelligence. ai.title: Trump helped build the Middle East’s AI ambitions. Could his war break them?
ai.description: President Donald Trump arrived in the Middle East last spring, making deals that would vault the Gulf into the global race for artificial intelligence.

ai.title: Looking for top-performing fund of FY26? Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund wins crown with 171%+ return
ai.description: Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund emerged as the top-performing mutual fund of FY26, delivering over 171% returns, driven by a strong semiconductor and AI-led rally. Experts caution against chasing past performance, advising investors to treat it as a high-risk satellite allocation within a diversified global portfolio.

ai.title: California AI order requires firms seeking state contracts to have safeguards against abuse
ai.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom signed an executive ​order on Monday ​that requires firms seeking contracts with ​the state to provide safeguards against AI misuse, including the generation of illegal content, harmful bias and violations of ‌civil rights.
